name: Vishvadevas
description: The Vishvadevas are a group of deities in Hindu mythology, often described as the 'All-Gods' or 'Universal Gods.' They are not a single deity, but rather a collective of ten gods, typically considered sons of Vishva, a daughter of Daksha Prajapati, and Dharma. They represent various aspects of cosmic order, moral law, and natural phenomena. Their roles vary across different texts, but they are generally associated with virtues, sacrifices, and auspicious beginnings. They are often invoked during rituals and ceremonies, particularly in the context of Shraddha (ancestral rites). The Vishvadevas are considered to be benevolent and helpful to humans, and their blessings are sought for prosperity and well-being. While not as prominent as other major deities like Vishnu or Shiva, the Vishvadevas play a significant role in the broader Hindu pantheon, embodying the interconnectedness of all things and the importance of dharma. They are considered to be intermediaries between humans and the higher gods, facilitating communication and ensuring the proper execution of religious duties. Their presence underscores the inclusive nature of Hinduism and the recognition of diverse divine manifestations. They are frequently worshipped alongside the Pitris (ancestors) during Shraddha ceremonies. The composition of the Vishvadevas can vary slightly depending on the specific text or tradition, but the concept of a collective of benevolent deities representing universal values remains consistent.
subtitle: The Universal Gods: Embodiments of Dharma

keyMyths.title: Origin of the Vishvadevas
keyMyths.description: The Vishvadevas are said to be the sons of Vishva and Dharma, representing different aspects of cosmic order. Their birth is a testament to the importance of dharma in the universe.

keyMyths.title: Vishvadevas in Shraddha
keyMyths.description: During Shraddha rituals, the Vishvadevas are invoked alongside the Pitris to ensure the successful offering to the ancestors. Their presence is crucial for the efficacy of the ceremony.

keyMyths.title: Blessings of the Vishvadevas
keyMyths.description: Worshipping the Vishvadevas is believed to bring prosperity, well-being, and the fulfillment of desires. They are considered benevolent deities who grant blessings to their devotees.

sources.author: Swami Venkatesananda
